messages.content: What does the DBHS website say about Test Scores & Performance?

CA Dashboard Diamond Bar High School (link to caschooldashboard.org).

To find detailed reports on Diamond Bar High School test results and scores, visit the California Department of Education DataQuest page: http://dq.cde.ca.gov/dataquest/

1. LEVEL = School
2. Choose test scores or other metrics you would like to see.
3. Click the SUBMIT button.
4. When asked for a portion of the school's name, type "Diamond Bar High".

Related: SARC (School Accountability Report Card) hosted at the district site: https://www.wvusd.org/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=54529&type=d&pREC_ID=898672

What do GLCs do at Diamond Bar High School?

GLCs handle conflicts, assist with class selection, explain college admission policies, provide personal guidance, and approve peer tutoring and short-term independent study contracts. They can be approached during Brunch or Lunch on campus.
Give me a detailed overview of Parent Groups ("Magnificent Seven").

The DBHS "Magnificent Seven" parent support groups:
1. Brahma Boosters
2. Brahma Foundation
3. Council of African American Parents (CAAP)
4. Chinese American Parent Association (CAPA)
5. Hispanic Organization for Parents and Education (HOPE)
6. Parents of India and Neighboring Countries (PINC)
7. Korean American Parent Association (KAPA)

These groups provide financial and volunteer support essential to the school.

Tell me about Student Clubs and Organizations.

DBHS has a large number of active student clubs for the 2025–26 school year. The official roster is maintained by USB. Jeremiah Wai updates the reference list. The full club list (with names, advisors, and meeting details) is in an embedded Google Sheet.

Barbara Pentangelo is the Brahma Tech Academy Coordinator in the Brahma Tech Academy / STEM department at DBHS.
Summarize Attendance Office.

Absence line: (909) 594-1860.

Attendance Clerks:
- Amber Luna — Attendance Clerk, students A–Lin — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33124
- Jennifer Chung — Attendance Clerk, students Lio–Z — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33123

Attendance FAQ

How to report an absence/tardy: Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods:
- Email — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason.
- Note — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature.
- Call the attendance office at the clerk extensions above.

Voicemail: If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording.

Incorrectly marked absent: Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Address discrepancies as soon as possible (before the end of each month). Students may pick up a slip fro

What grade would a student born in October 2010 be placed in for 2026–27?
A student born in October 2010 falls in the September 2, 2010 – September 1, 2011 range, so they would be placed in 10th grade (Class of 2029) for the 2026–27 school year.

What is Transcripts?

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

What are the key details about Awards & Recognition?

U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024).

US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation.

The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college.

Rankings (2024-25):
- #408 in National Rankings
- #56 in California High Schools
- #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools
- #149 in STEM High Schools

DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students.

The six ranking factors:

Tell me about International Baccalaureate (IB).

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations

Give me a detailed overview of History & Community.

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon

Tell me about Wellness Center.

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

Location: Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4.

Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

Tell me about the DBHS AP program.

AP Coordinator: Jennifer Burns. This page focuses on AP exam registration and payment logistics (it does not enumerate the individual AP courses offered).

AP Updates
- Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns.
- Use your personal email address and legal name when registering. If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it. College Board phone: (888) 225-5427, M–F 6am–3pm.
- Once registered in AP Classroom, pay for your exam through the DBHS Webstore. (Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.)

Important AP Dates (as published)
- September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557)
- November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee.
- March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee
Tell me about DBHS AND CTE - Alumni Spotlight - Abby Kottke.

Date: May 26, 2026
Source URL: https://dbhs.wvusd.org/apps/news/article/2202825?categoryId=1430

Congratulations to DBHS and CTE alum Abby Kottke (CO 2022) on graduation from CSULB and the presentation of their documentary films at the Full Spectrum 2026 Cinematic Showcase at the Carpenter Performing Arts Center.

Ms. Kottke is a recent graduate of the CSULB Cinematic Arts program. At DBHS she was a CTE pathway completer of the Digital Video Production program and the Brahma Cinematics Club President. She received a $500 scholarship in collaboration with the non-profit REACH for Mental Health for her Eye Opener documentary, Going Off Script Olivia James. (The Eye Opener category encourages students to see filmmaking as a form of social justice and awareness of social issues.)

Her films:
- ALIGNMENT — "An observation of a bonsai workshop and the various challenges students face." Director: Abby Kottke; Cinematographer: Kehan Liu.
- RIICHI REACH — "An observational portrait of the Kenko Mahjong Tournament at LAPOM, a LA Riichi Mahjong Club in Gardena, CA." Director: Kehan Liu; Cinematographer: Abby Kottke.
- FROM, FARMERSVILLE — "Farmersville, a small town, was
How do I explore careers and majors at DBHS?
Use Major Clarity at app.paper.co/majorclarity/ to explore careers and college majors. The Career Center (Room 265, Ms. Ayala) also offers one-on-one career counseling and job listings.
What does the DBHS website say about Suicide Prevention?

- By phone: National Suicide Prevention Hotline: (800) 273-8255 or 988 — https://suicidepreventionlifeline.org/
- By text: Text HOME to 741741 — https://www.crisistextline.org/
- Dial or text 988. 988 reaches the Lifeline — a network of more than 200 state and local call centers funded by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services through SAMHSA and administered by Vibrant Emotional Health. Service is provided in English and Spanish.

What is Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class.

How to Join: Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews.

Give me a detailed overview of Vision, Mission & SLOs.

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

Summarize Work Permits.

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section
 - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian
 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.
2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see Ms. Ayala in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days.

Work Permit Renewals
If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew.

Young Workers Health and Safety Information
Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish.

Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

Give me a detailed overview of Transcripts.

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

How do AP exams work at Diamond Bar High School?

AP Coordinator: Jennifer Burns. This page focuses on AP exam registration and payment logistics (it does not enumerate the individual AP courses offered).

AP Updates
- Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns.
- Use your personal email address and legal name when registering. If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it. College Board phone: (888) 225-5427, M–F 6am–3pm.
- Once registered in AP Classroom, pay for your exam through the DBHS Webstore. (Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.)

Important AP Dates (as published)
- September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557)
- November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee.
- March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee
How is the Pathways Academy schedule different from the regular schedule?
Pathways Academy students follow a block schedule on regular days. Blocks 7 and 8 (each 88 minutes) replace Periods 1–3. Period 4 starts at 11:52 AM, same as the regular schedule.
What should students know about Health Office?

- School Nurse: Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301
- Health Clerk: Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

What is Work Permits?

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section
 - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian
 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.
2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see Ms. Ayala in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days.

Work Permit Renewals
If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew.

Young Workers Health and Safety Information
Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish.

Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

What does the DBHS website say about Vision, Mission & SLOs?

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

What should students know about International Baccalaureate (IB)?

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

What should students know about Student Fees?

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How does the International Baccalaureate program work at DBHS?

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

What are the key details about Work Permits?

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section
 - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian
 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.
2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see Ms. Ayala in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days.

Work Permit Renewals
If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew.

Young Workers Health and Safety Information
Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish.

Summarize Grade Level Coordinators.

A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed.

What should students know about Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class.

How to Join: Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews.

What are the key details about Nondiscrimination Policy?

Diamond Bar High School prohibits discrimination, harassment, intimidation, or bullying in all district programs, activities, and employment on the basis of actual or perceived ancestry, age, color, disability, gender, gender identity, gender expression, nationality, race or ethnicity, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, parental or marital status, pregnancy, or association with a person or a group with one or more of these actual or perceived characteristics.
Summarize History & Community.

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon

What does the DBHS website say about Transcripts?

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

What is Student Fees?

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

What should students know about History & Community?

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Tell me about Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering).

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

What are the US News ranking factors for high schools?

US News ranks high schools on six factors: College Readiness (30%) — proportion of 12th graders earning qualifying AP/IB scores; College Curriculum Breadth (10%) — students earning qualifying scores across 4 content areas; State Assessment Proficiency (20%) — standardized test performance; State Assessment Performance (20%) — scores vs. predicted scores for similar demographics; Underserved Student Performance (10%) — outcomes for Black, Hispanic, and low-income students; and Graduation Rate (10%) — proportion of 9th graders from 2018–19 who graduated by 2022.

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

What are the key details about History & Community?

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon

Give me a detailed overview of Activities & United Student Body (USB).

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class.

How to Join: Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews.

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

Summarize Transcripts.

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

What should students know about Vision, Mission & SLOs?

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

What is Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Attendance FAQ

How to report an absence/tardy: Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods:
- Email — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason.
- Note — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature.
- Call the attendance office at the clerk extensions above.

Voicemail: If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording.

Incorrectly marked absent: Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Address discrepancies as soon as possible (before the end of each month). Students may pick up a slip fro

U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024).

US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation.

The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college.

Rankings (2024-25):
- #408 in National Rankings
- #56 in California High Schools
- #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools
- #149 in STEM High Schools

DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students.

What IB courses are offered at Diamond Bar High School?

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations

What are the demographics of Diamond Bar, California?

Diamond Bar has a population of 53857 (2021). The city is 53.1% Asian/Pacific Islander and 21.7% (down from 30% since 2014) white. About 43.3% of residents are foreign-born and 58.1% speak a language other than English at home. The median household income is $100,730.

What does the DBHS website say about Work Permits?

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

Can DBHS charge students fees?

No. The California Constitution mandates free public education. DBHS cannot require students to pay for educational activities, materials, supplies, or uniforms — whether curricular or extracurricular, for credit or not. A waiver process does not make a mandatory fee constitutional.
Explain Wellness Center at DBHS.

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

Location: Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4.

Explain Student Fees at DBHS.

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What is the mission of Diamond Bar High School?

Diamond Bar will value the five A's of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.
Give me a detailed overview of Awards & Recognition.

U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024).

US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation.

The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college.

Rankings (2024-25):
- #408 in National Rankings
- #56 in California High Schools
- #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools
- #149 in STEM High Schools

DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students.

Give me a detailed overview of Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering).

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college.

Rankings (2024-25):
- #408 in National Rankings
- #56 in California High Schools
- #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools
- #149 in STEM High Schools

DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

Attendance FAQ

How to report an absence/tardy: Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods:
- Email — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason.
- Note — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature.
- Call the attendance office at the clerk extensions above.

Voicemail: If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording.

Incorrectly marked absent: Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Address discrepancies as soon as possible (before the end of each month). Students may pick up a slip fro

Explain Transcripts at DBHS.

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

Summarize Student Fees.

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What should students know about Wellness Center?

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

Location: Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4.

Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

Explain International Baccalaureate (IB) at DBHS.

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations

What are the key details about International Baccalaureate (IB)?

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations
The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. Make c

AP Updates
- Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns.

What are the key details about Transcripts?

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

Summarize Wellness Center.

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

Location: Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4.

Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

What is Summer School 2026 — Overview at DBHS?

DBHS Summer School 2026 runs June 8 – July 16, 2026, Monday–Thursday each week. Classes are held 7:30 AM – 1:00 PM on campus (Mt. SAC classes run until 1:30 PM). Three programs are offered: WVUSD non-tuition remediation, Mt. SAC non-tuition advancement, and D2L tuition-based enrichment.

Explain Graduation Requirements at DBHS.

What is Grade Level Coordinators?

A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed.

Tell me about Transcripts.

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

What are the key details about Student Fees?

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What documents are required to enroll at DBHS?

Resident students living with parents must submit: 1. Verification of Residency Form A; 2. DMV-issued driver's license or California ID with current address; 3. Current utility bill or utility turn-on receipt (gas, water, or electric) for current address; 4. Tell me about History & Community.

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon
Explain Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering) at DBHS.

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

What is Wellness Center?

DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Vision Statement
The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources.

Location: Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4.

Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

What should students know about Work Permits?

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section
 - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian
 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.
2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see Ms. Ayala in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days.

Work Permit Renewals
If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew.

Young Workers Health and Safety Information
Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish.

Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

What should students know about Transcripts?

Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice.

To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact Mrs. Han if your request is not processed after 2 business days.

Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center.

Special Note for Current Seniors: Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment.

- Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com
- Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html

Explain Awards & Recognition at DBHS.

U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024).

US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation.

The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college.

Rankings (2024-25):
- #408 in National Rankings
- #56 in California High Schools
- #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools
- #149 in STEM High Schools

DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students.

What are the requirements to earn an IB Diploma at DBHS?

Students must complete one course from each of the 6 IB subject groups (3 subjects at HL and 3 at SL (or 4 HL and 2 SL). A student may only take a maximum of 2 SL courses in junior year.), write an Extended Essay, complete Theory of Knowledge, and meet CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service) requirements. A minimum 3.0 GPA in Groups 1–5 is required to enter and stay in the program.
Summarize International Baccalaureate (IB).

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations
The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. Make c

What does the DBHS website say about History & Community?

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon

Summarize Vision, Mission & SLOs.

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

No cost to student. CSF Peer Tutoring: students can come to get extra help in any subject from other students.


Peer Tutoring For a Fee

DBHS Teachers

Contact your student's teacher by leaving phone messages through the school switchboard at (909) 594-1405. Leave phone numbers and times you may be reached. How do I get a work permit at DBHS?

California law requires students under 18 to have a work permit. 1. Once hired by an employer, complete the Minor's Information section 2. Complete the School Information section 3. Have a parent or legal guardian sign the application 4. Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section 5. Turn in the completed form to Ms. Ayala in the Career Center (Room 265) Permits are processed in 1–2 business days by Ms. Ayala in the Career Center (Room 265).

What engineering programs are offered at DBHS?

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)
What should students know about Grade Level Coordinators?

A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed.
