Attendance FAQ

How to report an absence/tardy: Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods:
- Email — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason.
- Note — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature.
- Call the attendance office at the clerk extensions above.

Voicemail: If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording.

Incorrectly marked absent: Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Address discrepancies as soon as possible (before the end of each month). Students may pick up a slip fro
What is the DBHS Wellness Center?

The Wellness Center (Room 254, opened January 2016) is a confidential, nonjudgmental space for students seeking wellness support. Peer counselors help students develop healthy well-being strategies. Drop in during Periods 1–4.
What are the five A's and what do they mean?

The DBHS mission is built around five A's: Academics (rigorous, varied curriculum), Access (personalized programs for every student), Activities (student government, clubs, and events), Arts (visual and performing arts programs), and Athletics (competitive sports). Together they ensure every student has opportunities to discover their unique gifts.

What does the DBHS website say about Student Fees?

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

Give me a detailed overview of Work Permits.

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section
 - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian
 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.
2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see Ms. Ayala in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days.

Work Permit Renewals
If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew.

Young Workers Health and Safety Information
Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish.

What does the DBHS website say about Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Explain Vision, Mission & SLOs at DBHS.

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

Explain Activities & United Student Body (USB) at DBHS.

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class.

How to Join: Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org

Medication During School Hours
California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent.

Give me a detailed overview of Student Fees.

The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity.

This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity.

The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment.

A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible.

Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What is Vision, Mission & SLOs?

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

Tell me about Activities & United Student Body (USB).

Motto: "Raising the Bar"

United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets.

The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class.

How to Join: Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews.

Mission Statement
The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate.

Tell me about Work Permits.

Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW!

To apply for a work permit:
1. Once you have been hired by an employer:
 - Complete the Minor's Information section
 - Complete the School Information Section

 - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section.

Explain History & Community at DBHS.

Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools.

Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamon

What does the DBHS website say about International Baccalaureate (IB)?

The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.

The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives.

Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures.

Donations

What should students know about Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com.

Program Overview: Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders.

The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation.

Coordinators: Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Give me a detailed overview of Grade Level Coordinators.

A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed.

Tell me about Vision, Mission & SLOs.

Vision Statement

Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.

Mission

Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.
